description:
<!-- 
        TASK TITLE
        -----------
        Sort & uniq frequency counting for QA test results.
    -->

    You are a QA engineer preparing a small reporting utility.  
    The initial raw data file already exists on disk, but you must create a neatly
    formatted summary that management can audit automatically.

    STEP-BY-STEP OBJECTIVE
    ----------------------
    1. Make sure the directory “/home/user/qa_env/logs” exists.  
       (Create any missing parent directories as needed.)
    2. Read the file:
          /home/user/qa_env/test_results/raw_results.txt
       • Every line in that file is of the form  
           «module-name␣status»  
         where status is either PASS or FAIL.
    3. Using core GNU tools only (e.g. sort, uniq, etc.), build a frequency table
       that counts *identical* lines (i.e. the combination “module␣status”).
    4. Sort the frequency table
       • Primary key: count, in *descending* numeric order.  
       • Secondary key: the literal text (module␣status) in normal
         ascending ASCII order to break ties.
    5. Save the final report to
          /home/user/qa_env/test_results/summary.log

    REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT
    ----------------------
    • Each line must have exactly three fields separated by a single space:
          NN module-name status
      where
        NN  = a two-digit, zero-padded integer count (01-99).
        module-name = the exact module string from the raw file.
        status      = PASS or FAIL.
    • Do NOT add extra leading/trailing spaces.
    • Do NOT add headers or blank lines.
    • The lines must already be sorted as described in Step 4.

    AFTER YOU FINISH
    ----------------
    1. Display the full contents of “/home/user/qa_env/test_results/summary.log”
       to the terminal so a human reviewer can eyeball them.
    2. No other files should be modified or created.

    The automated checker will verify:
      a. That /home/user/qa_env/logs exists (a directory, not a file).
      b. That /home/user/qa_env/test_results/summary.log exists and is readable.
      c. That the content of summary.log exactly matches the format and sorted
         order described above.
truth:
<!--
        PRE-EXISTING TREE BEFORE THE AGENT STARTS
        -----------------------------------------
        /home/user/qa_env/
            test_results/
                raw_results.txt      (already on disk)
        All other directories do not yet exist.

        CONTENT OF raw_results.txt
        --------------------------
        auth PASS
        auth PASS
        auth FAIL
        payment PASS
        payment PASS
        payment PASS
        payment FAIL
        search PASS
        search FAIL
        search FAIL
        search FAIL
        report PASS
        report PASS
        report PASS
        report PASS

        EXPECTED CONTENT OF summary.log
        -------------------------------
        04 report PASS
        03 payment PASS
        03 search FAIL
        02 auth PASS
        01 auth FAIL
        01 payment FAIL
        01 search PASS
    -->
